title: Looking for Monday Developers
excerpt:
Hi - We are looking for a Monday.com development specialist to assist with the customisation and implementation of Monday into our business. 
We are looking for demonstrable (i.e. with examples/references) experience in:…

posts.author: peterrf (Peter Reed-Forrester)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi - We are looking for a Monday.com development specialist to assist with the customisation and implementation of Monday into our business.

We are looking for demonstrable (i.e. with examples/references) experience in:- API/Integration, Dashboard/Board design/development, workflow/automation design/development etc. etc.

We will need 3 professional proposals/quotes for the work and are happy to fully engage in initial discussions to facilitate this.

Please respond on here in the first instance.

Regards,
Peter Reed-Forrester
CEO Yorcoms Group Ltd

posts.author: rob (Roberto Tremonti)
posts.cookedHtml:
Welcome, @peterrf
I’m Roberto of Omnidea, monday.com Gold Partner and developer of General Caster monday.com app.
Feel free to contact me at roberto.tremonti@omnidea.it with some details on your request.

posts.author: Thomas-Omnitas (Thomas Karlsson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @peterrf

We at Omnitas Consulting are preferred premium monday.com partners. Located in Sweden, working world wide.

The information provided is a little on the thin side. In order for us to provide any kind of proposal/ quote.Please specify your use case, workflows and integration needs, either here or in an email to me (thomas@omnitas.se).
After you have provided the additional information we are happy to jump on a call and talk through your needs and show you relevant examples of what we have done for other clients.

With regards,

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @peterrf

Welcome to the community. Bas de Bruin form Excellent Team here, please have a look at our App Store (Downloads Archive - Excellent Team) and find the current monday.com apps we have build. As you might notice all our apps are integrations (no boards / dashboard views). I can do custom development, but I honestly think that creating more generic apps for a wider audience can bring down the costs considerably. We take the risk (and the reward) on the number of times apps are purchased through our store.

Leave me a PM or contact us at mailto:info@excellent-team.nl to discuss.

posts.author: Andrew1 (Andrew)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Peter,
If you are still on the lookout for some help, feel free to reach out to me as what you are wanting is well within my skill set.
You can reach me on andrewjohnson56782@gmail.com
Best Wishes,
Andrew

posts.author: jonkeable (Jon Keable)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Peter,

I’m astounded that you’re still trying to con people out of their work, and there’s a name for it now, it’s called ‘Job Fishing’. Basically, an unscrupulous person sets up a company in Companies House for a few quid, to make it look legitimate. And then they put an advert out on Facebook, for example, get people to do a months worth of work for you, because you pay people at the end of the month right?

But, of course they never get paid! The unscrupulous person takes the work they’ve done and disappears. Just like you’ve done with the negative search results for you on Google, they’ve disappeared! But, you now that they can still easily be seen, don’t you? And it doesn’t make for good reading.

Anyway Peter John Reed-Forrester, I’ve just had a look at you on Companies House and that’s a very complicated life you lead there. I’m surprised you’re still allowed to be a director.

Well, bad luck with your next venture Peter John Reed-Forrester, the worst thing you can steal from a person is their time. Someone who’d steal a part of someone’s life for their own gain is the lowest form of life. Stolen money can be returned, stolen things can be replaced or remade, but you can never return, replace or remake someones time. Once you’ve taken it, there’s no giving it back. It’s gone.

Try to live a good life Peter.

posts.author: Bianca (Bianca Clement)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m sorry this happened to you @jonkeable .
I thought there was something fishy about him…

I guess this is a good reminder to take a deposit for work, and ensure you still have control and sole knowledge until the bill is paid in entirety.

posts.author: jonkeable (Jon Keable)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Bianca,

For sure the majority of people are on the level and will do what they say they’ll do, their reputation relies on it. But, there’s some people that will game the system, which to be fair is very easy to do in the UK, being able to set up a Ltd company for few pounds is just one of them and being able to clean their history from Google etc another.

‘I guess this is a good reminder to take a deposit for work, and ensure you still have control and sole knowledge until the bill is paid in entirety.’

It shouldn’t be like that, but that’s what you have to do these days. Here’s a link to the BBC program that gave me the name for what this guy does:

BBC iPlayer

Jobfished

Catrin Nye investigates the glamorous company that faked its own existence.

Good luck, stay safe and I hope everything goes well for you.

Jonathan - @jonkeable

posts.cookedHtml:

welcome to the monday.com community.

Regards
Neelam B
